figures.caption: Figure 7: Singular values of the query, key, and value matrices involved in the attention computation for a subset of the layers and all attention heads. We use the Llama 3.2 (1B) model in float64 precision with the abstract of [Han+23] as input to demonstrate the singular value decays which are observed in the literature.
Figure 3: GPT architecture [Rad+18] - each layer of the GPT Decoder contains multiple submodules, only one of which is a masked Multi-Headed Attention submodule.
Figure 1: Example of sentence embedding for a word-based tokenizer for the phrase ’the lazy dog’.
Figure 10: Schematic depiction of the Linformer mechanism.
Figure 4: Overview of approximation techniques for self-attention.

Figure 2: Example of an attention layer for the embeddings obtained from the tokenization example in Figure 1. It is important to note that in this example we take the kernel to be linear for simplicity.
Figure 11: Schematic depiction of the Nyströmformer approximation.
Figure 9: Sketch of the symmetrization and approximation procedure.

Figure 5: Approximate sparsity pattern of the top 20 × 20 block of the masked attention matrix Z−1A ∈ R309×309 corresponding to theK andQmatrices given by the Llama 3.2 (1B) model with the HyperAttention [Han+23] abstract given as input, for different choices of heads and layers. The colors represent the magnitude of the entries, with lighter colors being larger elements; each row of the matrix has been normalized to have maximum element equal to 1. It is interesting to note that some heads in specific layers exhibit so-called attention sinks [Xia+24], visible as columns with consistently large weights, indicating tokens that attract attention across many query positions.
Figure 6: Ideally, the columns and rows of A can be reordered to produce a matrix that is close to block-diagonal. In practice, it is hard to guarantee that the blocks are comparably sized.

Figure 8: Singular values of different matrices involved in the attention computation for a subset of the layers and all attention heads. We use the Llama 3.2 (1B) model in float64 precision with the abstract of [Han+23] as input to demonstrate the singular value decays which are observed in the literature.
